titlestmt.titleproper: Preliminary Guide to the Collection of Bookplates from British Institutions
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Molly E. Dotson

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 10129652.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday September 1, 2020 at 23:05

did.unittitle: Collection of Bookplates from British Institutions
did.abstract: The collection comprises mostly 18th- and 19th-century bookplates from a variety of British institutions, including libraries, social organizations, military organizations, schools and colleges as well as businesses.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The Yale Bookplate Collection was formerly housed in Sterling Memorial Library, Room 608. The collection was incorporated into the Arts of the Book Collection in 1984 and then moved to the Seeley G. Mudd Library in 1990. In 2011 it was transferred to the Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has not been transferred to Yale University.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Collection of Bookplates from British Institutions, Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection was originally housed in two boxes labeled "English Institutions." Also, the series divisions are based on the original categories assigned to the collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection comprises mostly 18th- and 19th-century bookplates from a variety of British institutions, including libraries, social organizations, military organizations, schools and colleges as well as businesses.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into ten series: I. Parochial and Ecclesiastical Libraries. II. Circulating and Public Libraries. III. Clubs and Other Social Organizations. IV. Military Organizations. V. Schools and Colleges. VI. Premiums. VII. Universal Bookplates. VIII. Unidentified Bookplates. IX. Dr. John S. Pearson Collection of Bookplates from British Institutions.

Arranged alphabetically by the name of the institution. Whenever multiple bookplates from different organizations are affixed to the same mount, these are filed at the end of the series in a "Miscellaneous" folder or folders. Some of the miscellaneous items are duplicates of those appearing earlier in the series, whereas others are new to the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes bookplates from a variety of parochial and ecclesiastical libraries.

did.unittitle: Martin Benson, Bishop of Gloucester – York Minister

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes bookplates from both circulating libraries and municipal free public libraries.

did.unittitle: Manchester Circulating Library – Plymouth Proprietary Library
Reference Library (Buckingham Palace Rd.) – Worcester Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Booksellers often offered circulating library services.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder also contains a plate for Adolphe Art Photographer and Miniature Painter, 75 Grafton St., Dublin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes bookplates from a wide variety of social organizations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a record of subscribers' names as well as dates for when a book was delivered by and returned to the Godalming Book Society.

did.unittitle: Louth Book-Club – Society for the Propagation of the Gospel in Foreign Parts
Society of Antiquaries of London – Working Men's Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains an ex-libris design for the Royal Watercolour Society. The design features a landscape with a monument in the foreground, listing members' names and capped with a palette labeled "Water Colours."

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous clubs and other social organizations
Miscellaneous clubs and other social organizations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a membership list in the order of circulation along with dates for the Castle-Gate Junior Book-Society.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous clubs and other social organizations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a bookplate for the Ex Libris Lodge, No. 3765, a Freemason organization founded in London in 1915 by bookplate enthusiasts. This bookplate was designed by J. A. C. Harrison.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous clubs and other social organizations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes bookplates from a variety of military organizations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes bookplates from a variety of schools and colleges, ranging from grammar school to university.

did.unittitle: Blackheath Proprietary School – Presbyterian College of England

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes prize bookplates – also known as premiums. Schools often presented students with books as a reward; starting in the 18th century, these books typically included a bookplate inscribed with the name of the student and the subject or other reason for recognition.

did.unittitle: The Association Incorporated for Discountenancing Vice and Promoting the Knowledge & Practice of the Christian Religion – St. Peter's College (Westminster, London, England)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes universal bookplates, which are ex-libris designs that are typically available commercially and allow the owner to insert his or her own name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes mostly unidentified armorial bookplates, although it also includes a few unidentified pictorial ex-libris designs as well.

did.unittitle: Dr. John S. Pearson Collection of Bookplates from British Institutions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes a group of British institutional bookplates housed in an envelope addressed to Dr. J. S. Pearson.
